differential_diagnoses: Disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC) secondary to venomous snakebite
Acute kidney injury (acute tubular necrosis) secondary to snakebite envenomation
next_steps: Remove the tourniquet and assess distal pulses/perfusion
Administer appropriate antivenom as indicated for systemic envenomation
Urgent stabilization including fluid resuscitation and blood component therapy (packed RBCs, fresh frozen plasma, and platelets)
Consult obstetrics for urgent evaluation of fetal distress and placental abruption
Monitor urine output closely with a Foley catheter
Obtain type and crossmatch, coagulation profile, fibrinogen levels, and electrolytes
Prepare for emergent cesarean section if indicated by worsening maternal status or fetal distress
